project.title: Decoding Octopus Mucus: Impact of Aquarium-Maintenance and Senescence on the Skin Mucus Proteome Profile of the Common Octopus (Octopus vulgaris)
project.projectDescription: This work investigates the comparison of protein profiles in the Octopus vulgaris skin mucus of wild, aquarium-maintained, and senescent specimens to identify potential welfare biomarkers, using tandem mass tag (TMT) in an Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer to create a reference dataset from octopus skin mucus obtained in different conditions.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: A total of 6 specimens of O. vulgaris were used and maintained individually in tanks under different conditions (wild, aquarium-maintaied and semescent). Skin mucus samples were collected and protein extracts were prepared. A total of 100 µg of skin mucus protein extract were digested with trypsin and labeled with TMT 10-plex labeling. Samples were acidified with formic acid and analyzed HCD by a liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) using a Vanquish™ Neo UHPLC system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA, USA) coupled to an Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) equipped with a field asymmetric ion mobility spectrometry (FAIMS) Pro unit.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The mass spectra were analyzed by SEQUEST-HT (Protein Discoverer 2.4, Thermo Fisher Scientific). Results were validated using a FDR <1%.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Institute of Marine Research (IIM-CSIC), Vigo, Spain
labPIs.name: Mónica Carrera

references.referenceLine: Pérez-Polo S, Mena AR, Barros L, Borrajo P, Pazos M, Carrera M, Gestal C. Decoding Octopus Skin Mucus: Impact of Aquarium-Maintenance and Senescence on the Proteome Profile of the Common Octopus (Octopus vulgaris). Int J Mol Sci. 2024 25(18):9953

Characteristics[Specimen]: Skin mucus samples were collected from octopus specimens using the method of Vizcaino et al. (2023).
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: A total of 6 specimens of O. vulgaris with an average weight of 1 kg (980–1400 g) were collected using fishing cages by professional certificated fishermen at the Ría de Vigo, Spain (2414.090 N, 847.180 W) and were transported in containers to the Experimental Culture Facilities of IIM-CSIC, an institution registered as a “User and breeding center on animal experimentation” ES360570202001.
Characteristics[SamplingTime]: after 10 min of arms exposure to the air
Characteristics[Temperature]: The water temperature was set at around 15 ± 1 °C
Characteristics[Label]: TMT 10-plex labeling was used (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[Instrument]: Vanquish™ Neo UHPLC system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA, USA)
Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) equipped with a field asymmetric ion mobility spectrometry (FAIMS) Pro unit
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: followed by 10 data-dependent higher energy collision dissociation (HCD) MS/MS scans (1 µscan)
Comment[SpecilizedMS]: field asymmetric ion mobility spectrometry (FAIMS) Pro unit
FAIMS compensation voltages (C.V.) alternated between −40, −60, and −80 V within a single run
Comment[Separation]: reversed-phase chromatography using a C18 column (EASY-Spray column, 25 cm, 75 µm ID, PepMap C18, 2 µm particles, 100 Å pore size, Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[GradientTime]: A 120 min linear gradient from 1 to 45% B
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: up to 2 possible missed cleavage sites
